description: Truths within truths are still truths.

hints.text: The goal is $(A \land B) \land C \to B$. $B$ is buried two layers deep inside a nested [conjunction|conjunction]. Start by assuming everything on the left side.
First, peel off the outer layer with `and-elim-l`. This gives you $A \land B$ — the inner [conjunction|conjunction].
Now the inner layer. You want $B$, which is the **right** half of $A \land B$. Use `and-elim-r` on your new step.
$B$ is reached. Discharge the original [assumption|assumption] to get the [implication|implication].
